properties.name: African Elephant
properties.description: The African elephant is the largest land animal on Earth. Tierpark Berlin houses a small herd in a spacious outdoor enclosure.

properties.name: African Elephant
properties.description: Outdoor enclosure for the African elephant herd.

properties.name: African Lion
properties.description: The king of the savannah. Tierpark Berlin's lions can be observed in their large outdoor pride area.

properties.name: African Lion
properties.description: Outdoor pride area for the lions inside the Alfred-Brehm-Haus complex.

properties.name: Giraffe
properties.description: Giraffes are the tallest living terrestrial animals. Tierpark Berlin's giraffes live in a modern facility with outdoor access.

properties.name: Western Lowland Gorilla
properties.description: The western lowland gorilla is critically endangered. Tierpark Berlin participates in the European breeding programme.

properties.name: Hippopotamus
properties.description: Hippos spend most of their time in water to keep their skin moist. The hippo house at Tierpark Berlin offers underwater viewing.

properties.name: Amur Tiger
properties.description: The Amur tiger is the largest subspecies of tiger, adapted to cold climates. Tierpark Berlin supports conservation efforts for this endangered cat.

properties.name: Humboldt Penguin
properties.description: Humboldt penguins are native to the coasts of South America. The Tierpark colony can be watched swimming in their rocky outdoor pool.

properties.name: Polar Bear
properties.description: Polar bears are the world's largest land predators. Visitors can watch Tierpark Berlin's polar bears playing in their large pool.

properties.name: Plains Zebra
properties.description: Plains zebras are known for their distinctive black-and-white stripes. They roam a large grassy paddock at Tierpark Berlin.

properties.name: Plains Zebra
properties.description: The African Savannah exhibit shared with other grazing animals.

properties.name: Chilean Flamingo
properties.description: Chilean flamingos are recognisable by their pink plumage. Tierpark Berlin's flock lives in a large open lagoon.

properties.name: Red Panda
properties.description: Red pandas are shy, tree-dwelling mammals from the eastern Himalayas. Their forested enclosure at Tierpark Berlin replicates their mountain home.
